deep_dive.title: Binocular Gaze Estimation with Single Camera and Single Light Source
deep_dive.abstract: According to commonly consented theories, the minimum hardware requirement for gaze tracker is one camera and two light sources to realize gaze estimation with free head movements. However, in some scenarios such as eye tracking on mobile devices, it is preferable to use less components, especially light sources. We propose a gaze estimation method with one camera and one light source. A "virtual light source" is introduced, which is geometrically placed symmetrically to the real light source with respect to the camera, and generates a "virtual glint" in the acquired image. We estimate the "virtual glint" by exploiting the relationship between the distance between two pupils and two glints in the captured image, and estimate the gaze with polynomial regression assuming two light sources are available. A new normalization factor for regression method is verified, which turns out to be practical for one-glint system. The performance is proved to be acceptable, while degradation is noticed compared to system with two actual light sources.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes a gaze estimation method using minimal hardware, directly applicable to gaze-based HCI
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出一种使用最少硬件的注视估计方法，直接适用于基于注视的人机交互
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出一种使用最少硬件的注视估计方法，直接适用于基于注视的人机交互
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出一种使用最少硬件的注视估计方法，直接适用于基于注视的人机交互
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces a method for binocular gaze estimation with only one camera and one light source, reducing hardware requirements while maintaining accuracy, which is crucial for mobile and accessible gaze-based interfaces.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种仅用单摄像头和单光源即可实现双目注视估计的方法。通过引入虚拟光源和瞳孔-光斑距离关系，解决了传统需要双光源的硬件限制。实验证明该方法在自由头部运动下仍具较高精度，为移动设备等资源受限场景中的注视交互提供了经济可行的方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一种仅用单摄像头和单光源即可实现双目注视估计的方法。通过引入虚拟光源和瞳孔-光斑距离关系，解决了传统需要双光源的硬件限制。实验证明该方法在自由头部运动下仍具较高精度，为移动设备等资源受限场景中的注视交互提供了经济可行的方案。

quick_skim.title: Judging the reasons for fixations: A direct experimental method to assess the contribution of saliency and semantic factors to gaze control
quick_skim.abstract: Current debates regarding the relative contribution of saliency versus semantics to gaze control often rely on comparing the predictive power of saliency and meaning maps. We argue that such indirect, global approaches are fundamentally limited because fixations arise from heterogeneous, local causes that are conflated in whole-scene comparisons. To substantiate this claim, we used a direct method where participants explicitly identified the reasons for fixation at specific clusters of high fixation density, distinguishing between low-level saliency and various semantic categories, as well as the most important one. The obtained judgments revealed that multiple factors contribute simultaneously to gaze control. Although their influence varied across fixation clusters, semantics generally dominated saliency. Notably, abstract semantic categories, particularly "unknown/unusual," proved important, highlighting the role of prior knowledge and novelty besides personal relevance in guiding attention. To interpret these findings in the context of existing models, we propose a framework distinguishing between processes highlighting interesting locations in the image from a sampling strategy translating this information into scanpaths. Within this framework, classic saliency and meaning maps are viewed as restricted inputs to the strategy, whereas deep learning-based models (e.g., DeepGaze IIE) are more general and may also implicitly encode aspects of the strategy itself. Consistent with this, we found that the predictive performance of DeepGaze IIE varied less significantly with the specific reasons for fixation than that of classic saliency and meaning map approaches.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: assesses saliency vs semantic factors in gaze control, useful for computational gaze models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估注视控制中显著性vs语义因素，对计算眼动模型有用
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估注视控制中显著性vs语义因素，对计算眼动模型有用
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估注视控制中显著性vs语义因素，对计算眼动模型有用
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces a direct method to judge reasons for fixations, distinguishing saliency from semantics, which can inform computational models of human gaze for intent prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种直接实验方法，让受试者明确判断注视点的原因（显著性vs语义类别），揭示多种因素共同驱动注视。该方法为构建更精确的眼动预测模型提供了经验基础，有助于开发基于眼动的人类意图理解系统。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一种直接实验方法，让受试者明确判断注视点的原因（显著性vs语义类别），揭示多种因素共同驱动注视。该方法为构建更精确的眼动预测模型提供了经验基础，有助于开发基于眼动的人类意图理解系统。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: computational models for understanding human goals through eye movements

quick_skim.title: Humans use optimal eye movements to facilitate mental rotation of objects
quick_skim.abstract: The ability to mentally rotate objects is a fundamental feature of human cognition, and humans can use this ability to make choices about objects based on their geometry. However, remarkably little is known about how such choices are reached, and what sort of visual information might facilitate them. We devised an experiment where participants had to mentally simulate an object's rotation to choose which of two objects was better for a subsequent task based on its shape alone. We also tracked their gaze while they made their choice, to see which visual information they were using to facilitate this mental simulation. We found that participants were consistently able to choose the most suitable object for the task, and, remarkably, the visual information they sampled was directly linked to their choices. Put simply, participants made better choices when they looked at more informative regions of the objects, and participants who sampled regions that were better for facilitating mental simulation made better choices overall. These findings reveal a direct link between fixations, simulation, and decision-making, suggesting that to perform any fine-grained mental simulation people need to direct their gaze at specific, informative points of an object to simulate its two-dimensional proximal image displacement.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies optimal eye movements during mental rotation to understand cognitive goals
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究心理旋转过程中的最优眼动以理解认知目标
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究心理旋转过程中的最优眼动以理解认知目标
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究心理旋转过程中的最优眼动以理解认知目标
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper demonstrates that humans use optimal eye movements to facilitate mental rotation, providing a computational link between gaze patterns and cognitive goals, directly relevant to understanding human intentions via eye tracking.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文通过眼动追踪实验，发现受试者在心理旋转任务中会策略性地选择注视点以辅助认知操作。结果表明眼动模式与内部认知目标高度关联，为基于眼动理解人类意图的计算模型提供了实证基础，可启发前瞻性交互系统中的人机眼动协同。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文通过眼动追踪实验，发现受试者在心理旋转任务中会策略性地选择注视点以辅助认知操作。结果表明眼动模式与内部认知目标高度关联，为基于眼动理解人类意图的计算模型提供了实证基础，可启发前瞻性交互系统中的人机眼动协同。
